title: Minimize secret exposure
description: Keep credentials, tokens, keys, and signing material out of source, logs, errors, telemetry, prompts, generated artifacts, and broad process environments.
body: Use when code reads configuration, builds logs, handles exceptions, emits telemetry, packages artifacts, or calls external services. Do not use to decide incident response or key rotation policy by itself. Actions: avoid printing secret-bearing values, narrow environment propagation, prefer references over raw values, and add redaction before the sink rather than after emission. Validation signals include tests or snapshots showing redacted outputs, repository searches for committed material, and artifact inspection. Risk boundary: observed secret exposure is an incident signal and must be escalated for human rotation and containment.
